title: M365 standalone UCP pack surface
execution_plan_ref: Operations/EXECUTION_PLAN.md § Initiative: M365 standalone UCP pack surface

principles: keep M365 service/runtime ownership inside the M365 repo instead of leaving the UCP-facing pack source embedded in UCP
preserve truthful fail-closed service-mode execution and JWT-backed actor identity
provide a bounded standalone first-party pack surface that UCP can consume through the public SDK contract
anti_alignment: does not weaken the M365 service auth contract
does not add new Microsoft tenant capabilities
does not move UCP host logic into this repo

user_stated_requirements: clean out the old embedded UCP M365 path
move the M365 integration pack to the correct standalone place
intent_capture.intent_verification: R1 through R5 create the standalone UCP-facing M365 pack surface in this repo so UCP can consume it without embedding the pack source locally.

objective: Create the standalone first-party UCP-facing M365 pack surface in the M365 repo so UCP can load the pack from its owner repo, while preserving service-mode-only live execution and removing any need for UCP-local pack ownership.
current_state: The M365 service/runtime is correct and accepted, but the UCP-facing m365_pack source still lives in the sibling UCP repo. This repo does not yet expose a standalone `m365_pack` module/package that UCP can load directly as the owner repo.
target_state: The M365 repo owns a UCP-facing standalone pack package with contracts and client modules, the live executor path is service-mode only plus explicit test stub behavior, and focused tests/documentation prove the package is the authoritative source for `m365_pack`.

in_scope: create the governed package for the standalone UCP-facing M365 pack surface
add a standalone `ucp_m365_pack` package in this repo
port the UCP-facing pack contracts and client logic into this repo
remove direct-import fallback from the live executor path
add focused tests and docs for the standalone pack surface
update M365 governance trackers and project file index
out_of_scope: editing sibling UCP repo files in this plan
file_denylist: Any path not listed in the allowlist
scope.scope_fence_rule: Stop and re-scope if this slice requires UCP-side code changes or Microsoft tenant changes.

requirements.description: The M365 repo must have notebook-backed governance evidence and a formal plan/prompt package for the standalone UCP pack surface.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The plan triplet, prompt pair, notebook evidence, and verification artifact exist and validate cleanly.
requirements.user_intent_trace: move the M365 integration pack to the correct standalone place

requirements.description: This repo must expose a standalone `ucp_m365_pack` package containing the UCP-facing pack contracts.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The package exports `create_pack_adapter`, `risk_tier_for_agent`, and confirmation helpers for the M365 pack surface.
requirements.user_intent_trace: clean out the old embedded UCP M365 path

requirements.description: This repo must expose a standalone `ucp_m365_pack.client` module for UCP-facing action execution and validation.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The client supports service-mode execution plus explicit stub mode and no longer contains a direct-import live fallback.
requirements.user_intent_trace: clean out the old embedded UCP M365 path

requirements.description: Focused tests and docs must prove this repo is now the authoritative owner of the UCP-facing M365 pack surface.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Focused tests are green and a commercialization doc explains the standalone-pack boundary.
requirements.user_intent_trace: move the M365 integration pack to the correct standalone place

requirements.description: Governance closure must update M365 trackers and leave the branch committed and pushed cleanly.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Operations trackers are truthful, git diff --check is clean, and the branch is committed and pushed.

tasks.name: Lock the governed package
tasks.description: Create the M365-side plan/prompt package and notebook-backed governance proof for the standalone UCP pack surface.
exit_criteria: The package is present in md/yaml/json form and the prompt pair exists.
tasks.validation_command: ../UCP/.venv/bin/python -c "import json, pathlib, yaml; from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator; schema=json.loads(pathlib.Path('../UCP/agent_governance/PLAN_TEMPLATE_SCHEMA.json').read_text()); data=yaml.safe_load(pathlib.Path('plans/m365-ucp-standalone-pack-surface/m365-ucp-standalone-pack-surface.yaml').read_text()); Draft202012Validator(schema).validate(data); print('plan schema ok')"

tasks.name: Add standalone contracts package
tasks.description: Create the UCP-facing standalone pack contract module in this repo.
exit_criteria: The package exports the pack adapter factory and contract helpers.

tasks.name: Add standalone client package
tasks.description: Create the service-mode-only UCP-facing client module in this repo.
exit_criteria: The client supports service-mode plus explicit stub mode and omits direct-import live fallback.

tasks.name: Document and validate authoritative ownership
tasks.description: Add focused docs and test proof that this repo now owns the standalone UCP-facing pack surface.
exit_criteria: The doc explains the standalone-pack/service-mode boundary.

tasks.name: Close governance and ship cleanly
tasks.description: Update Operations trackers and project file index, then commit and push the branch cleanly.
exit_criteria: The branch is committed and pushed.

checks.name: Plan schema validation
checks.description: The plan must validate against the canonical UCP plan schema.
checks.validation_command: ../UCP/.venv/bin/python -c "import json, pathlib, yaml; from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator; schema=json.loads(pathlib.Path('../UCP/agent_governance/PLAN_TEMPLATE_SCHEMA.json').read_text()); data=yaml.safe_load(pathlib.Path('plans/m365-ucp-standalone-pack-surface/m365-ucp-standalone-pack-surface.yaml').read_text()); Draft202012Validator(schema).validate(data); print('plan schema ok')"

checks.name: Prompt contract validation
checks.description: The detailed and kickoff prompts must validate under the governed prompt contract.
checks.validation_command:
PYTHONPATH="../UCP/src:src" ../UCP/.venv/bin/python - <<'PY'
from ucp.governance.artifact_contracts import validate_prompt_artifact
for path in [
    'docs/prompts/codex-m365-ucp-standalone-pack-surface.md',
    'docs/prompts/codex-m365-ucp-standalone-pack-surface-prompt.txt',
]:
    verdict = validate_prompt_artifact(artifact_path=path)
    assert verdict['valid'] is True, verdict
    print(path, verdict['verdict'])
PY"

checks.name: Focused standalone pack tests
checks.description: Focused standalone pack tests must pass before closure.
checks.pass_criteria: Focused standalone pack coverage is green.

decision.go_rule: The M365 repo exposes the standalone UCP-facing pack surface with service-mode-only live execution and focused validation is green.
decision.no_go_rule: The package still depends on direct-import live fallback or focused validation is red.

no_go_triggers: The standalone client still exposes direct-import live fallback.
The standalone contracts or client package cannot be imported from this repo.
Focused standalone pack tests are red.

artifacts.validation: YAML artifact validates against the canonical schema.
JSON artifact mirrors the YAML plan.
Detailed prompt validates under the governed prompt contract.
Kickoff prompt points to the detailed prompt and preserves the bounded scope.
Notebook-backed governance prerequisite exists for the standalone pack package.
Verification artifact records plan and prompt package readiness.
Canonical doc explains the standalone-pack/service-mode ownership boundary.

external_data: sibling UCP repo at ../UCP for public SDK imports during focused tests
environment.pre_notebook_check: This slice uses notebook-backed governance evidence only; no long-running notebooks are expected.

options_considered.description: Keep the UCP-facing pack source embedded only in the UCP repo and document the ownership split informally.
pros: No new package in this repo.
cons: Leaves the wrong ownership model in place.

options_considered.description: Create a standalone `ucp_m365_pack` package in this repo and make it the authoritative owner of the UCP-facing M365 pack surface.
pros: Matches the intended standalone first-party pack model.
Keeps live service-mode ownership in the M365 repo.
cons: Requires cross-repo focused validation with the public SDK contract.

implementation_approach.rationale: The cutover only becomes truthful if the M365 repo actually owns the UCP-facing pack source. A standalone `ucp_m365_pack` package here gives UCP a clean owner-repo target and removes the need for embedded M365 pack code inside UCP.

rejected_rationale.A: Informal documentation does not fix the wrong source-ownership boundary.

items.risk: The standalone package could drift from the already-accepted service-mode auth contract.
items.mitigation: Port only the UCP-facing pack surface, keep service-mode-only live execution, and keep the auth contract unchanged.

items.risk: Focused tests may require the public SDK import path from the sibling UCP repo.
items.mitigation: Use bounded cross-repo PYTHONPATH during focused tests only.

hard_blockers: The standalone package cannot be imported cleanly from this repo.

procedure: Revert only the files created by this slice.
Remove the standalone package and related focused tests/docs if validation does not pass.
governance_updates_on_rollback: Remove this initiative entry from Operations/EXECUTION_PLAN.md and Operations/ACTION_LOG.md.

task_checklist.evidence: Plan triplet, prompt pair, notebook evidence, and verification artifact are present.
Standalone client package exists at src/ucp_m365_pack/client.py with service-mode-only live execution.
Focused tests and commercialization doc prove this repo owns the standalone UCP-facing pack surface.
Operations trackers are updated and the branch is prepared for clean commit/push closure.

when_blocked.format: STATUS: BLOCKED / TASK: {id} / REASON: {desc} / MISSING: {list} / NEXT ALLOWED ACTION: {path}

agent_constraints: Do not weaken the M365 service-mode auth contract.
Do not perform sibling UCP edits in this slice.
Do not add new Microsoft action domains.

validation_summary: The M365 repo now owns the authoritative UCP-facing standalone pack surface.
Focused standalone-pack validation is green (13 passed).
closure_summary.next_dependency_boundary: Sibling UCP consumer cutover is complete; no further dependency remains inside this standalone-pack ownership slice.
